question: how do i get a water bucket in minecraft
answer: You can collect water by using an empty bucket on a water source block or a full water cauldron.

question: does depth strider actually help with water currents
answer: Yes, the Depth Strider enchantment increases your water movement efficiency by one-third. This boost also reduces the speed at which flowing water pushes you when you are standing still.

question: why does my water source block sometimes disappear when i try to make an infinite pool
answer: Water source blocks form when a flowing block is horizontally adjacent to two or more other source blocks and is sitting on a solid block or another source. If you are in Java Edition, check if the game rule waterSourceConversion is set to false, as this disables the creation of new source blocks. Also, ensure your setup allows the water to flow into a third block from two distinct source directions to properly trigger the formation of a new, renewable source.

question: how do i get lava into a bucket
answer: You can collect lava by using a bucket on a lava source block or a full lava cauldron. This will convert your empty bucket into a lava bucket.

question: can i make an infinite lava source in survival
answer: In standard survival gameplay, you cannot create new lava source blocks by placing them next to each other like you can with water. However, you can create a renewable supply by placing a lava source above pointed dripstone, which will slowly fill a cauldron with lava over time.

question: why does my armor break so fast when i fall in lava
answer: Lava deals damage to both you and your gear, and certain armor types are specifically vulnerable to it. While wearing armor enchanted with Fire Protection can help you survive longer, non-netherite armor pieces will still take durability damage while you are burning. If you have enough Fire Protection enchantments, you can actually survive the damage indefinitely as long as your hunger bar is full, but your armor will continue to degrade until it breaks.

question: how do i make a villager get a job
answer: To give a villager a profession, you need to place a job site block, such as a lectern or composter, near an unemployed villager. In Java Edition, the villager will pathfind to the block to claim it, while in Bedrock Edition, they will claim it if it is within a 16-block radius.

question: can i change a villager's profession after i have already traded with them
answer: No, once you have made your first trade with a villager, their profession becomes locked permanently. They will keep that profession forever, even if you break or remove their job site block.

question: why do my villagers keep losing their jobs and becoming unemployed
answer: Villagers lose their jobs if they cannot reach their claimed job site block after several attempts. If a villager fails to pathfind to their workstation, they will show angry particles, and eventually, the block becomes unclaimed. If there are no other nearby job sites, the villager will remain unemployed until they can find and successfully reach a new workstation.

question: how do i craft a pickaxe
answer: you can make a wooden, stone, copper, iron, gold, or diamond pickaxe by combining two sticks and three of the corresponding material in a crafting table.

question: can i repair my pickaxe in an anvil
answer: yes, you can repair a pickaxe in an anvil by using the same material it is made of. each unit of material you add will restore 25% of the tool's maximum durability.

question: why did my pickaxe break instantly when mining gold ore
answer: if you use the wrong tier of pickaxe, the game applies a penalty to the breaking time and you will not get any items. gold ore specifically requires at least an iron pickaxe to harvest; if you try to mine it with a wooden or stone one, you will not receive the ore block.

question: how do i change my skin in minecraft
answer: In Java Edition, you can change your skin by uploading a PNG image file in the Minecraft Launcher or on the preferences page of minecraft.net. In Bedrock Edition, you can use the Character Creator menu found on the title screen or pause menu to customize your appearance.

question: why does my hunger bar go down so fast when i run
answer: The hunger bar depletes faster during energy-intensive activities like sprinting, jumping, attacking mobs, and mining. If your hunger bar is at 17 or lower, you will stop regenerating health, and if it hits 0, you will start losing health.

question: how does the game calculate my movement speed when walking or sprinting
answer: Movement speed is determined by base acceleration countered by friction from the block you are walking on. For walking, the base acceleration is 0.098, and sprinting is 30% faster. Each tick, your velocity increases by the acceleration value and is then multiplied by the block's friction, which is 0.546 for most blocks. This creates a balance where the friction eventually counteracts the acceleration, resulting in a steady speed of about 4.317 m/s for walking and 5.612 m/s for sprinting.

question: do zombies drop anything besides rotten flesh?
answer: Yes, zombies can drop iron ingots, carrots, potatoes, and baked potatoes, though these are rare drops. They also have a guaranteed drop of a zombie head and the Lava Chicken music disc if specific conditions are met.

question: can i stop a zombie from turning into a drowned?
answer: No, once a zombie begins the conversion process by being submerged in water for 30 seconds, it cannot be stopped. Even if the zombie leaves the water after the shaking starts, it will still transform into a drowned after 15 seconds.

question: how do zombie reinforcements work on hard mode?
answer: When a zombie is damaged while targeting a player or entity, it has a chance to spawn additional zombies to help. It will attempt to find a spawn location within a range of 7 to 40 blocks away that has a light level of 0 and a solid surface. These reinforcements can spawn even in biomes where zombies don't normally appear, like mushroom fields, but they will not spawn if the game rule doMobSpawning is turned off.

question: how do i make a bedrock dedicated server
answer: You can download the server software for free from the official download page. The software is distributed as a ZIP archive that contains the executable and an initial server.properties file.

question: can i use the /reload command on my bedrock server
answer: No, the /reload command is not supported on Bedrock Dedicated Servers. If you change your server settings or add new plug-ins, you must restart the server for the changes to take effect.

question: why does my server lag when i have a lot of entities around
answer: Bedrock Dedicated Server performance can be poor on multi-core server CPUs that have low clock speeds. If you have a large number of entities or complex chunks loaded, the server might experience a situation where CPU usage appears low, but the entire server becomes unresponsive. Using a desktop processor with higher single-core performance is generally more effective for running a server under high load.

question: how do i make a chest in minecraft
answer: You can craft a chest by placing any type of wooden planks into all eight outer slots of a crafting table, leaving the center slot empty.

question: can i put two chests next to each other without them turning into a large chest
answer: Yes, you can place two small chests side by side without them joining. In Java Edition, hold the sneak key while placing the second chest. In Bedrock Edition, you can push a chest into place using a piston, or simply place the second chest facing a different direction than the first.

question: why wont my chest open when i click it
answer: There are a few reasons a chest might not open. First, check if a cat is sitting on top of the chest, as this prevents it from opening. Second, ensure there is no solid block directly above the chest in Java Edition, or any slab above it in Bedrock Edition, as these block access. Finally, if you are playing on a server, the chest might be locked with a specific item requirement, or you may be in a location where piglins have become hostile toward you for interacting with their storage.

question: how do i make a skeleton stop burning in the sun
answer: Skeletons will stop burning if they are in water, in a shaded area with a light level of 11 or less, or if they have a non-air block directly over them. They also will not burn if they are wearing a helmet.

question: do skeletons drop anything different if i use a looting sword
answer: Yes, using a Looting enchantment increases the maximum quantity of bones and arrows dropped by a skeleton. For example, in Java Edition, Looting III increases the maximum drop count to 5 and the average drop count to 2.5.

question: why do skeletons sometimes shoot faster when i get close to them
answer: In Bedrock Edition, skeletons are programmed to increase their rate of fire as you get closer to them, which helps them knock you back and makes melee combat more difficult. The fire rate can speed up from once every 3 seconds to once every 1 second as you approach. Additionally, if you are in water, they will switch to melee attacks because they cannot use their bows while submerged.

question: how do i make a village in minecraft?
answer: You cannot manually craft a village, as they generate naturally in specific biomes like plains, savanna, taiga, meadows, snowy plains, and deserts. However, you can create your own by placing beds and job site blocks to attract and house villagers.

question: why did my villager stop restocking their trades?
answer: Villagers need to be linked to a specific job site block to restock. If they are employed but not linked to their job site, they cannot restock, and they also cannot link to a block that has already been claimed by another villager.

question: how does the game decide where the center of a village is?
answer: In Bedrock Edition, the center is defined by the pillow of the first bed a villager links to. If the village boundaries shift as more villagers link to different points of interest, the center usually moves to the midpoint between the furthest blocks in each direction. In Java Edition, there is no single center; instead, the village is defined by any subchunk containing at least one claimed bed, bell, or job site block.

question: how do i make a pickaxe to mine stone?
answer: To craft a pickaxe, you need to use the crafting grid. You place three planks or other materials in the top row and two sticks in the middle column below them.

question: why does it take so long to break blocks when im swimming?
answer: Mining speed is significantly reduced when your head is underwater and your feet are not touching the ground. You receive a 5x penalty for being underwater and an additional 5x penalty for not standing on solid ground, making you 25x slower than on land.

question: is it possible to instant mine stone in survival?
answer: Yes, you can instant mine stone if your tool's damage per tick exceeds the block's hardness multiplied by 30. For stone, which has a hardness of 1.5, you need a damage value greater than 45. This is achievable by using an Efficiency V netherite or diamond pickaxe combined with the Haste II status effect.

question: how do i craft a block of emerald
answer: you can craft a block of emerald by placing 9 emeralds into a crafting grid.

question: can i use fortune on emerald ore
answer: yes, you can use fortune on emerald ore. it will drop an extra emerald per level of fortune, giving you a maximum of 4 emeralds if you have fortune iii.

question: why do my emerald trades get more expensive
answer: villager trades have a price multiplier that affects how the cost changes. when you trade repeatedly, the price can increase, but you can lower it back down by getting the hero of the village effect or by curing a zombie villager.

question: how do i craft arrows in minecraft
answer: You can craft arrows by placing one flint, one stick, and one feather in a crafting grid. This recipe will yield four arrows.

question: why cant i pick up the arrows i shot at my friend
answer: Arrows that are stuck in a player cannot be retrieved, and the player does not drop them when killed. Additionally, arrows shot by a player using an Infinity-enchanted bow cannot be picked up.

question: do arrows shot by skeletons or pillagers ever despawn if i dont pick them up
answer: Yes, arrows that stick into blocks will remain there for one minute before disappearing. If you do not pick them up within that time, they will despawn. Note that if an arrow is already stuck in a block, the one-minute timer will refresh if it is interacted with or if the chunk is reloaded.

question: how do i make planks in minecraft?
answer: you can craft planks by placing a log, stem, stripped log, stripped stem, wood, or hyphae into your crafting grid. you can also craft them using a block of bamboo or a block of stripped bamboo.

question: can i use crimson planks as fuel in a furnace?
answer: no, crimson and warped planks cannot be used as fuel in a furnace. however, you can craft them into sticks, which are then usable as fuel.

question: why do my planks break faster when i use an axe?
answer: planks have a specific hardness value, and using an axe is the correct tool for the job. while you can break them by hand, using an axe significantly reduces the time it takes to mine them. for example, while hand-breaking takes 2 seconds, using a wooden axe reduces that to 0.75 seconds, and a netherite axe brings it down to 0.25 seconds.

question: how do i make a creeper explode on purpose
answer: You can force a creeper to explode by using a flint and steel or a fire charge on it.

question: do creepers still explode if i hit them and run away
answer: A creeper will only explode if it has an uninterrupted line of sight with you during its 1.5-second countdown. If you move out of its sight or get more than 7 blocks away, the detonation will be canceled.

question: why did the creeper explode when it fell on me
answer: Creepers have a special behavior where taking fall damage adds to their internal swell timer. If a creeper falls from a height greater than 16 blocks, the fall damage can trigger the explosion almost immediately upon landing, as the swell value increases by the fall distance multiplied by 1.5.

question: how do i get wool from a sheep
answer: You can obtain 1–3 wool blocks by using shears on a sheep. This does not damage the sheep, and the wool will regrow after the sheep eats a grass block.

question: why wont my sheep regrow their wool
answer: Sheep need to graze on grass blocks to regrow their wool. If there is no grass available for them to eat, they will remain sheared indefinitely.

question: does looting make sheep drop more mutton
answer: Yes, the Looting enchantment increases the amount of raw or cooked mutton dropped by a sheep. While a sheep normally drops 1–2 mutton, a Looting III sword increases the drop to 1–5 mutton, with an average of 3.00 per kill.

question: how do i craft a bed in minecraft?
answer: To craft a bed, you need to place three matching wool blocks in the top row of a crafting table and three wooden planks of any type in the middle row.

question: why cant i sleep in my bed even though it is night?
answer: You might be unable to sleep if there are monsters within 8 blocks horizontally or 5 blocks vertically of the bed head. Additionally, if you are playing in Java Edition, you must be within 3 blocks of the bed, or within 2 blocks if you are playing Bedrock Edition.

question: what happens if i try to use a bed in the nether or the end?
answer: Attempting to sleep in a bed in the Nether or the End causes it to explode, which deals damage and sets fire to surrounding blocks. The explosion has a power of 5, which is stronger than standard TNT, and it centers specifically on the head part of the bed. While this is dangerous for players, villagers can sleep in these dimensions without triggering an explosion.

question: how do i make a crafting table in minecraft
answer: You can craft a crafting table using the 2x2 grid in your inventory. It is one of the basic blocks you can create to help you build tools and other items.

question: can i play minecraft on my laptop and my friends console together
answer: Yes, if you are both playing Bedrock Edition, you can play together across different platforms. Java Edition is generally limited to PC, but Bedrock Edition supports cross-platform multiplayer between devices like Windows, consoles, and mobile.

question: does changing my skin size in bedrock edition make me harder to hit
answer: No, changing your skin size in Bedrock Edition does not change your actual hitbox size. While it might make you visually smaller or larger, your physical presence in the game remains the same, though it could still be an advantage in multiplayer because it might be harder for other players to see you.

question: how do i tame a wolf in minecraft
answer: You can tame a wolf by feeding it bones. Each bone has a 1/3 chance of successfully taming the wolf.

question: can i put armor on my wolf and how do i take it off
answer: Yes, you can equip wolf armor by using it on a tamed wolf. If you need to remove the armor, you can use shears on the wolf.

question: why do my wolves sometimes teleport to me and sometimes just sit there
answer: Tamed wolves teleport to you if they are more than 12 blocks away, provided they are not sitting, in a boat, in a minecart, or leashed to a fence. They also will not teleport if you are not touching the ground, such as when flying with elytra or swimming. If a sitting wolf is injured by another player, it may stand up and teleport to you to defend you, then sit back down afterward.

question: how do i turn a villager into a witch?
answer: You can transform a villager into a witch by having it struck by lightning. This can happen naturally during a thunderstorm or by using a trident with the Channeling enchantment to strike the villager directly.

question: do witches despawn if they are in their swamp hut?
answer: Witches that spawn inside a swamp hut during world generation never despawn. However, for any witches that spawn in the hut after the world is already generated, they will follow standard mob behavior unless they are part of a raid.

question: why do witches sometimes survive lava or fire for so long?
answer: Witches have a 15% chance to drink a potion of Fire Resistance if they take fire damage or are currently on fire. Because they also frequently drink potions of Healing when not at full health, they can survive for 40 to 50 seconds even while taking constant damage. They usually only die in lava if they choose to drink a healing potion first instead of the fire resistance one.

question: how many people can play on a bedrock realms core server at once
answer: A Realms Core subscription in Bedrock Edition allows up to 4 players to be active in a world at the same time.

question: can i use my own world with custom experimental settings on a realm
answer: No, worlds on Realms cannot use experimental settings. However, you can bypass this by uploading a local world that already has those options enabled to the Realm.

question: what happens to my manual backups if i run out of storage space on my bedrock realm
answer: Realms Core subscriptions provide 5GB of storage for manual saves, while Realms Plus subscriptions provide 10GB. These manual backups are retained as long as the Realm exists, but because the storage is limited, you will need to manage your saves to avoid hitting that capacity. You can check your current usage and manage these files in the general Realm settings screen.

question: how do i get leaf blocks in my inventory without them disappearing?
answer: To keep leaf blocks as items, you must break them using shears or a tool enchanted with Silk Touch. If you break them with any other tool, they will not drop as blocks.

question: why do my leaves keep vanishing after i chop down the tree?
answer: Leaves decay when they are not connected to a log or wood block. In Java Edition, they check for a connection within 6 blocks, while in Bedrock Edition, the limit is 4 blocks. If you place the leaves yourself, they will never decay.

question: does using fortune on leaves actually help me get more saplings?
answer: Yes, the Fortune enchantment increases the drop rates for saplings and sticks. For example, the base chance for a sapling from most leaves is 5%, but with Fortune III, that chance increases to 10%. Note that leaves destroyed by fire will not drop any items at all.

question: how do i make a boat in minecraft?
answer: You can craft a boat by placing five matching Overworld planks in a U-shape in a crafting grid. Note that crimson and warped planks cannot be used for this.

question: can i put a chest on my boat and how do i do it?
answer: Yes, you can create a boat with a chest by placing a chest and a matching boat together in a crafting grid. This allows you to transport items along with you while you travel.

question: why is my boat moving so much faster on blue ice than on water?
answer: Boats move faster on specific surfaces because of their friction coefficient. Water has a friction value of 0.9, which limits the top speed to 8 blocks per second. Blue ice has a much lower friction value of 0.989, which allows the boat to reach a top speed of over 72 blocks per second. Because the boat applies a deceleration based on these surface values, the lower friction on ice lets the boat maintain a much higher velocity.

question: how do i breed pigs in minecraft
answer: You can breed pigs by feeding them carrots, potatoes, or beetroots. Once fed, they will enter love mode and produce a baby pig.

question: can i ride a baby pig if i put a saddle on it
answer: No, you cannot ride baby pigs. Baby pigs cannot be saddled, and even if you manage to saddle one, you are unable to mount it.

question: how does the speed boost work when riding a pig with a carrot on a stick
answer: When you use a carrot on a stick while riding, the pig enters a boost state that consumes 7 durability. The pig accelerates to a peak speed of 5.2 blocks per second, then gradually slows back down to its default speed of 2.42 blocks per second by the end of the boost duration. The average speed during this entire cycle is roughly 4.19 meters per second.

question: how do i make an iron ingot
answer: You can obtain iron ingots by smelting iron ore or raw iron in a furnace or blast furnace. You can also craft them by placing 9 iron nuggets in a crafting grid.

question: do i get more iron from zombies if i use looting
answer: Yes, using a sword with the looting enchantment increases the drop chance of iron ingots from zombies, zombie villagers, and husks. While the default drop chance is 0.83%, it increases to 1.83% with looting III.

question: how much health does an iron golem get back when i use an iron ingot on it
answer: Using an iron ingot on an iron golem restores 25 health points. This is a useful way to keep your golems alive if they have taken damage from combat or other sources.

question: how do i make planks out of logs
answer: You can craft planks by placing any log or stem into your crafting grid. One log or stem will produce four planks.

question: can i use stems to make charcoal
answer: No, stems cannot be smelted into charcoal. Only logs can be used as a smelting ingredient to create charcoal in a furnace.

question: is there any difference between logs and stems for fuel
answer: Yes, there is a functional difference. While logs can be used as fuel in a furnace to smelt 1.5 items per block, stems cannot be used as fuel at all. Additionally, stems are not flammable in the same way, as they are categorized as non-flammable wood.

question: how do i make sticks in minecraft
answer: You can craft sticks by placing two planks in a vertical column in your crafting grid. You can also craft them using bamboo, where two bamboo items will yield one stick.

question: can i get sticks from breaking leaves with a silk touch axe
answer: No, you cannot get sticks from leaves if you use a tool enchanted with Silk Touch. Normally, leaves have a 2% chance to drop 1–2 sticks when they decay or are broken, but Silk Touch prevents this drop.

question: does looting actually help when farming sticks from witches
answer: Yes, the Looting enchantment significantly improves your chances of getting sticks from witches. While the default drop rate is around 33.49% in Java Edition, using Looting III increases the average number of sticks dropped per kill to 1.43. This is because the enchantment increases both the quantity and the probability of the drops occurring per kill.

question: how do i get an ender pearl
answer: You can get ender pearls by killing endermen. They have a 50% chance to drop one when killed by a player or a tamed wolf.

question: why do endermen keep teleporting away when i try to hit them
answer: Endermen teleport when they take damage from players, projectiles, or natural sources. If you hit them, they usually try to teleport a few blocks behind you to avoid further damage.

question: can i stop an enderman from teleporting if i trap it
answer: Endermen need at least three non-solid blocks above their destination to successfully teleport. If you remove all valid teleport locations within their range, you can prevent them from escaping. However, they cannot teleport while inside a boat or minecart, which is a common way to keep them stationary.

question: how do i make a furnace in minecraft
answer: You can craft a furnace by placing eight cobblestone blocks into a crafting table in a square shape, leaving the center slot empty.

question: what happens if i mine cobblestone without a pickaxe
answer: If you try to break cobblestone without using a pickaxe, it will take significantly longer to mine and the block will not drop anything at all.

question: why does my cobblestone generator sometimes make stone instead of cobblestone
answer: A cobblestone generator works by having flowing lava touch water. If the lava flows on top of the water from above, it creates stone instead of cobblestone. To get cobblestone, you must ensure the lava is a flowing stream when it hits the water, as a lava source block will turn into obsidian upon contact with water.

question: how do i stop piglins from attacking me in the nether
answer: You can stop piglins from being hostile toward you by wearing at least one piece of golden armor.

question: do piglins attack if i open a chest near them
answer: Yes, opening or breaking a container like a chest, barrel, or shulker box will provoke nearby piglins. They will become aggravated if you perform these actions within a certain range, even if you are wearing gold armor.

question: why did my piglin stop bartering and start attacking me after i hit it
answer: When you damage a piglin, it enters a state of heavy aggravation. In this state, the piglin is unable to barter and will remain hostile for 30 seconds. You cannot distract a piglin with gold while it is under heavy aggravation, so it will continue to attack until the timer runs out.

question: how do i craft a slab in minecraft
answer: You can craft slabs using various materials, such as wood planks or stone, though the specific recipe depends on the block type. Slabs are non-standard blocks that occupy less than a full cubic meter of space.

question: can i walk up a block that is taller than one meter
answer: You can automatically step up onto a block if the height difference is at most 0.6 of a block. Most solid blocks are exactly 1 meter high, so you cannot step up onto a full block from a flat surface without jumping.

question: why do some blocks turn into entities when i break the block underneath them
answer: Most blocks stay put, but specific ones like sand, gravel, anvils, and concrete powder are affected by gravity. When you remove the block supporting them, they lose their static position and become entities that fall until they land on a solid surface or hit an invalid space.

question: how do i spawn the wither?
answer: To spawn the wither, place four blocks of soul sand or soul soil in a T-shape and put three wither skeleton skulls on top of the three upper blocks. The last block placed must be one of the skulls.

question: can the wither break obsidian or other strong blocks?
answer: Yes, the wither is the only mob that can destroy obsidian. When it takes damage, it breaks all blocks in a 3x4x3 area around it, though it cannot break certain special blocks like bedrock, end portal frames, or reinforced deepslate.

question: why does the wither stop taking damage from my arrows halfway through the fight?
answer: When the wither drops below half health, it gains a natural effect that makes it immune to projectiles like arrows and thrown tridents. This effect stays active as long as its health remains below half, but it will disappear if the wither manages to regenerate health back above that point.

question: how do i turn on subtitles in java edition
answer: You can enable closed captions in Java Edition by going to the 'Accessibility' section of the options menu, or through the 'Music & Sounds' options.

question: what do the little arrows next to the text mean in closed captions
answer: The '<' or '>' symbols indicate the direction a sound is coming from. They appear when the sound source is located off-screen.

question: why do some sounds have specific names like rabbit squeaks but others just say block broken
answer: Closed captions provide specific labels for entities, such as 'Rabbit squeaks' or 'Villager mumbles', to help you identify the source. However, for generic actions like placing or removing blocks, or the sound of footsteps, the game does not specify the exact block type and instead uses a general label like 'Block broken' or 'Footsteps'.

question: how do i make green dye in minecraft
answer: You can obtain green dye by smelting a cactus in a furnace with any fuel source.

question: can i redye terracotta or concrete after i already colored it
answer: No, once terracotta or concrete powder has been stained with a dye, it cannot be redyed to a different color. You would need to craft new blocks using the desired dye color from the start.

question: why cant i get a green lamb by breeding a blue sheep and a yellow sheep
answer: While dyeing sheep and breeding them generally follows color mixing rules, the game does not allow blue and yellow sheep to produce a green lamb. If the parent colors cannot be mixed, the game simply picks one of the two parent colors at random with an equal chance for the lamb.

question: how do i turn a zombie villager back into a normal villager?
answer: You need to apply the Weakness effect to the zombie villager using a splash potion, lingering potion, or arrow of weakness. Once weakened, use a golden apple on it to start the curing process.

question: do zombie villagers despawn if i leave them alone while they are curing?
answer: No, they do not despawn if you move far away while they are in the process of converting. However, they will still despawn if you change the world difficulty to Peaceful.

question: is there any way to speed up the time it takes to cure a zombie villager?
answer: Yes, you can speed up the conversion by placing iron bars or beds near the zombie villager. The game checks for these blocks in a 9x9x9 cube centered on the mob, and having up to 14 of these items within that space can decrease the countdown timer by an average of 4.2%.

question: how do i craft a dispenser
answer: You can craft a dispenser by placing one bow and one redstone dust around seven cobblestone blocks in a crafting table. The bow can be at any level of durability.

question: what happens if i mine a dispenser without a pickaxe
answer: If you mine a dispenser without using a pickaxe, you will not get the block back. It will only drop the items that were stored inside its inventory.

question: why does my dispenser sometimes not fire when i power it
answer: Dispensers have a four game tick delay between activation and firing, and they only trigger on the rising edge of a redstone signal. This means they do not continue to eject items while powered; you must use a clock circuit to repeatedly activate the block if you want it to fire multiple times. Additionally, in Java Edition, you might be encountering quasi-connectivity, where the block acts as if it is powered by a signal reaching the space above it.

question: how do i make an axe in minecraft?
answer: You can craft an axe by placing 3 units of your chosen material (like planks, iron ingots, or diamonds) and 2 sticks in a crafting table. The recipe can be flipped horizontally, so you can place the materials on either the left or right side of the grid.

question: does using an axe to strip logs or scrape copper use up durability?
answer: Yes, using an axe to strip logs or scrape oxidation and wax off copper blocks consumes 1 point of durability. However, if you are playing on Java Edition and holding a shield in your off-hand, you will need to sneak to perform these actions instead of just blocking.

question: how exactly does the shield disabling work when i hit someone with an axe?
answer: When you hit a target that is actively holding their shield up with an axe, you perform a crushing blow that disables their shield for 5 seconds. This effect applies to all of the target's shields and triggers regardless of your attack cooldown percentage. Note that this does not work if the target's shield is still within its 250ms activation delay, and performing the blow does not consume any durability from your axe.

question: how do i make a sign in minecraft?
answer: You can craft a sign by combining matching planks and a stick in a crafting grid. This recipe produces 3 signs at once.

question: can i stop people from changing the text on my signs?
answer: Yes, you can lock a sign by using a honeycomb on it to wax it. Once a sign is waxed, it cannot be edited or unwaxed unless you break it and place it down again.

question: why does my sign text look different when i face east or west?
answer: If you dye a sign, the text appears brighter and more saturated when the sign faces east or west compared to north or south. This happens because the game's lighting engine applies side lighting to the world to prevent it from looking flat, but this lighting does not affect the text itself, making the sign face appear dimmer in certain orientations.

question: how do i craft a piston in minecraft
answer: To craft a piston, you need to combine any type of wooden planks, cobblestone, an iron ingot, and redstone dust in a crafting table.

question: can pistons push chests and other containers
answer: In Java Edition, pistons cannot push blocks that hold block entities, such as chests, hoppers, or furnaces. However, in Bedrock Edition, these blocks can be pushed by pistons.

question: why does my piston sometimes take longer to activate than other times
answer: This happens because of the start delay. In Java Edition, the delay is 0 ticks if the piston is powered during the scheduled, random, or block event phases, but it becomes 1 tick if powered during the entity or block entity phases. Because blocks pushed by pistons arrive during the block entity phase, a chain of pistons pushing redstone blocks to trigger the next one will result in activations occurring 3 ticks apart.

question: how do i make a potion in minecraft
answer: you need to use a brewing stand. every potion starts with a water bottle, which you then brew with various ingredients to give it effects.

question: what happens if i drink a potion while i already have the effect
answer: drinking a potion while you are already under the same effect will not add to the duration. instead, it just resets the timer back to the full duration of that potion.

question: why does my regeneration potion heal for less time when i upgrade it to level 2
answer: enhancing a potion with glowstone dust trades some of its total duration for a stronger effect. for example, a base regeneration potion lasts 44 seconds, but the level ii version only lasts 22 seconds because it heals you more health per tick during that shorter window.

question: how do i light a fire in minecraft
answer: You can place fire by using either flint and steel or a fire charge on a block.

question: why did my wooden house burn down even though i had cobblestone between the fire and the roof
answer: Fire can spread to flammable blocks up to four blocks above the original fire source. Because fire ignores intervening blocks like cobblestone, it can easily ignite a wooden roof if it is within that vertical range.

question: how long do i actually stay on fire after i walk out of the flames
answer: Players have a Fire tag value that starts at -20 ticks, meaning you can spend up to 1 second inside fire and leave without continuing to burn. However, if your Fire value exceeds 0, it is immediately set to 160 ticks, and you will continue to burn for that duration after leaving the fire source.

question: how do i breed chickens in minecraft
answer: You can breed chickens by feeding them wheat seeds, beetroot seeds, melon seeds, pumpkin seeds, torchflower seeds, or pitcher pods. Once you feed two chickens, they will enter love mode and produce a chick, which also grants you 1–7 experience orbs.

question: do chickens take fall damage if they fall from a high place
answer: No, chickens are immune to fall damage. They flap their wings while in midair to fall slowly, which prevents them from taking any damage regardless of the height they fall from.

question: why did my egg not spawn a chicken when i threw it
answer: Throwing an egg only has a 1/8 chance of spawning a single chick. It is not guaranteed to work every time, so you often need to throw multiple eggs to get one to hatch. If you are lucky, there is also a very rare 1/256 chance that an egg will spawn four chicks instead of just one.

question: how do i tame a horse in minecraft
answer: To tame a horse, you need to mount it repeatedly until it stops bucking you off and hearts appear above it. You can increase your chances by feeding the horse to raise its temper.

question: can i take the armor off my horse if i change my mind
answer: Yes, you can remove horse armor by using shears on the horse. If both a saddle and armor are equipped, the first use of the shears will remove the armor, and a second use will remove the saddle.

question: why does my horse have less health than the wiki says
answer: If your horse has an odd number of health points, the game does not display the final half-heart in the health bar. For example, a horse with 15 health will only show 14 health points on the bar, which can make it look like it has taken damage when it is actually at full capacity.

question: how do i make a furnace in minecraft?
answer: You can craft a furnace by placing eight blocks of cobblestone, blackstone, or cobbled deepslate in a crafting table, leaving the center slot empty.

question: what happens if i take out the fuel while my stuff is still smelting?
answer: If the fuel runs out before an item finishes processing, the smelting progress pauses. The process will resume once you add more fuel to the furnace.

question: if i put coal in the furnace but only smelt one item, does it waste the rest of the coal?
answer: Yes, it wastes the remaining fuel. Once a unit of fuel starts burning, it continues until it is fully consumed, even if the upper slot is empty or the item finishes processing early. For example, a piece of coal burns for 80 seconds regardless of whether you process one item or eight.

question: how do i make a torch in minecraft
answer: You can craft a torch by placing one stick and one piece of coal or charcoal in a crafting grid. This recipe yields 4 torches.

question: do torches melt ice and snow
answer: Yes, torches emit a light level of 14 and will melt snow layers within 2 blocks and ice within 3 blocks. This distance is measured using taxicab distance.

question: why do my sand blocks break when they fall on my torches
answer: Gravity-affected blocks like sand and gravel break if they fall onto a solid block that has a torch sitting on top of it. However, if there is an air block directly under the torch, the falling blocks will pass through the torch space and land on the surface below instead of breaking. This happens because torches are non-solid and do not have a collision box, so they cannot support the weight of falling blocks.

question: how do i craft an anvil?
answer: To craft an anvil, you need 31 iron ingots in total. You use three blocks of iron and one iron ingot in the crafting grid.

question: can i repair my diamond pickaxe with diamonds in an anvil?
answer: Yes, you can repair a single item using the same material it was crafted from. Each material used will restore 25% of the item's maximum durability.

question: why does my anvil keep saying too expensive when i try to combine enchantments?
answer: Anvils have a limit of 39 levels for any operation. Every time you repair or combine items, the experience cost doubles, and once the cost exceeds 39 levels, the anvil will refuse the operation. This limit exists to prevent infinite upgrading, though it does not apply in Creative mode.

question: do baby animals drop xp when i kill them?
answer: No, baby animals do not drop any experience orbs when killed. This also applies to villagers, bats, and golems.

question: if i use a silk touch pickaxe on diamond ore, do i still get the xp?
answer: No, you do not get experience if you mine diamond ore with a Silk Touch pickaxe. You only get the experience when you place the block back down and mine it with a regular pickaxe to release the mineral.

question: how does the game decide which xp orbs to drop when i kill a mob?
answer: When a mob dies, the game calculates the total experience amount and then splits it into specific orb sizes, starting with the largest possible values first. The available orb sizes are 1, 3, 7, 17, 37, 73, 149, 307, 617, 1237, and 2477. For example, if a mob drops 1000 experience, the game will drop it as a 617 orb, a 307 orb, a 73 orb, and a 3 orb.

question: what do spiders drop when you kill them?
answer: Spiders drop string and spider eyes when killed. They also drop 5 experience orbs if killed by a player or a tamed wolf.

question: do spiders attack you if it's bright outside?
answer: Spiders are neutral mobs, so they only attack if the light level around them is 11 or less, or if you attack them first. If they chase you into a brighter area, they will return to a neutral state unless you hit them.

question: why do spiders sometimes have potion effects on them?
answer: In Java Edition, spiders have a chance to spawn with Speed, Strength, Regeneration, or Invisibility effects when playing on Hard difficulty. This chance is based on the local difficulty of the area, and if applied, the effect lasts forever. If a spider gets the Invisibility effect, you can still see its eyes.

question: how do i check what pack format my game is using?
answer: You can find the pack format by typing /version in the game chat or by pressing the F3 + V debug hotkey.

question: why does my resource pack say it is incompatible?
answer: A resource pack will show as incompatible if the format number inside its pack.mcmeta file is higher than the version your game currently supports. This system is in place to prevent crashes or missing content that can happen when using packs built for newer game versions.

question: how do i make a pack that works for both 1.21.9 and older versions?
answer: For Minecraft 1.21.9 and newer, you should use the min_format and max_format fields in your pack.mcmeta file instead of the old pack_format field. By setting these two fields, you can define a range of supported formats that allow the same pack to function across multiple game versions without needing separate files.

question: how do i make glass in minecraft
answer: You can make glass by smelting sand in a furnace using any fuel source.

question: what happens if sand falls on a torch
answer: If falling sand lands in a space occupied by a non-solid block like a torch, the sand will break and turn into an item instead of placing as a block.

question: why does my sand disappear when i try to build with it
answer: Sand is affected by gravity and will fall until it hits a solid block. If it lands on a non-solid block like a slab, rail, or torch, it will break into an item. Additionally, if the sand entity exists for more than 600 ticks, or if it is outside the building height, the game will delete the entity to prevent performance issues.

question: how do i craft a minecart?
answer: You can craft a minecart by placing five iron ingots in a U-shape pattern in a crafting table.

question: how do i get my minecart back after i place it?
answer: You can retrieve a minecart by attacking it with a pickaxe, sword, or axe. In Java Edition, make sure your attack cooldown is reset before you hit it, or it won't drop as an item.

question: why do my minecarts stop moving when i go far away?
answer: Minecarts become frozen if they travel more than one chunk beyond your current simulation distance. They will stay in that state until you get close enough to them again, at which point they will resume their movement.

question: how do i make a slime spawn in my world
answer: Slimes spawn naturally in specific 'slime chunks' below Y=40, or in swamp biomes between Y=51 and Y=69 when the light level is 7 or less. You can also spawn them using a slime spawn egg in Creative mode or by using the /summon command.

question: why wont slimes spawn in my swamp farm at night
answer: Slime spawning in swamps depends on the moon phase; they never spawn during a new moon and appear most often during a full moon. Additionally, the game checks if the light level is 7 or less, so ensure your farm area is sufficiently dark to meet these conditions.

question: do slime chunks work the same way in bedrock and java
answer: No, they function differently. In Java Edition, slime chunks are determined pseudo-randomly based on the world seed combined with the chunk coordinates. In Bedrock Edition, the algorithm is not dependent on the seed, meaning slime chunks are located at the exact same coordinates in every world.

question: how do i make the yellow text on the title screen change?
answer: The yellow text, known as a splash, is chosen randomly from a list every time you open the game. You cannot manually change it in-game, but you can modify the list of splashes by editing the respective file in a resource pack.

question: are splash texts the same in every language?
answer: No, splash texts are not translated. They appear the same regardless of the language setting you have chosen in the game.

question: where are the splash text files located in java edition?
answer: In Java Edition, the splash texts are defined in a plain-text file located within the minecraft.jar file. Specifically, you can find them at /assets/minecraft/texts/splashes.txt.

question: how do i find a trial chamber in my world?
answer: You can find a trial chamber by purchasing a trial explorer map from a journeyman-level cartographer villager for 12 emeralds and a compass.

question: do trial spawners always spawn the same mobs?
answer: No, when a trial chamber generates, one mob from a specific category is randomly selected for the spawners in that structure. This means you might get different mob types like zombies, spiders, or skeletons depending on the specific trial chamber you enter.

question: why are some trial spawners in my chamber not spawning breezes?
answer: Trial spawners are configured to spawn mobs based on the category selected during the structure's generation. While breezes are in their own category and guaranteed in certain chambers, other spawners are randomly assigned to melee, small melee, or ranged categories, which never include breezes.

question: how do i make a vindicator spawn in my world
answer: You cannot craft or build a vindicator. They spawn naturally in woodland mansions or during raids, but you can also spawn them using a spawn egg or a command.

question: do vindicators attack you if you find them in a mansion
answer: It depends on how they spawned. Vindicators generated in woodland mansions are sometimes neutral and will only attack you if you hit them first, but those that spawn during raids are always hostile.

question: why did my shield stop working when fighting a vindicator
answer: Vindicators use an iron axe to attack, and their axe strikes have the ability to disable your shield for 5 seconds. This happens even if the vindicator is riding a ravager, making them one of the most dangerous melee mobs in the game. Because they deal such high damage, you should be careful when approaching them without a way to avoid their initial hits.

question: how do i make coarse dirt
answer: you can craft coarse dirt by combining two dirt blocks and two gravel blocks in a crafting grid.

question: does fortune help get more flint from gravel
answer: yes, using a shovel enchanted with fortune increases your chances of getting flint. while a normal shovel has a 10% chance to drop flint, fortune i gives you about a 14.3% chance, fortune ii gives 25%, and fortune iii guarantees a 100% drop rate.

question: what happens if gravel falls on a torch
answer: if a block of falling gravel lands in a space occupied by a non-solid block like a torch, rail, or redstone dust, the gravel block will break instantly. when it breaks this way, it drops itself as an item, but it will never drop flint. this is a common way to clear out large piles of gravel quickly without having to mine every single block.

question: how do i get bad omen now
answer: You get the Bad Omen effect by drinking an ominous bottle, which is always dropped by a pillager captain when it dies.

question: can i use a shield to stop pillager arrows
answer: Yes, you can block any projectile, including arrows fired by pillagers, by using a shield. This also completes the 'Not Today, Thank You' advancement.

question: do pillager crossbows break if i use them
answer: In Java Edition, a pillager's crossbow will eventually break after 465 uses unless it has the Unbreaking enchantment. However, in Bedrock Edition, the crossbows held by pillagers do not break at all.

question: how do i make a zombie turn into a drowned?
answer: You need to keep a normal zombie's head submerged in water for 30 seconds. It will start shaking, and 15 seconds later, it will finish converting into a drowned.

question: do drowned spawn in rivers during the day?
answer: Yes, drowned can spawn in rivers during the day. Unlike in ocean biomes or dripstone caves where they only spawn below Y=58, this restriction does not apply to rivers as long as the water is deep enough for the sky light level to reach 7 or lower.

question: why is it so hard to get a trident from a drowned?
answer: Only drowned that spawn naturally with a trident in their hand can drop one, and the drop chance is quite low. In Java Edition, a drowned holding a trident has an 8.5% base chance to drop it, which increases by 1% per level of Looting up to a maximum of 11.5%. Because of these odds, you would need to kill many drowned to have a high statistical probability of obtaining one.

question: how do i make wool in minecraft
answer: You can obtain wool by using shears on an adult sheep, which yields 1–3 blocks. Alternatively, you can craft it using 4 pieces of string in a square pattern in your crafting grid.

question: does looting or fortune help when shearing sheep
answer: No, neither the Fortune nor the Looting enchantments affect the amount of wool you get from shearing a sheep. Shearing an adult sheep will always yield between 1 and 3 blocks regardless of your tool's enchantments.

question: why do sculk sensors not react when i walk on wool
answer: Sculk sensors are designed to be unable to detect specific actions involving wool, including entities walking, sprinting, or jumping on top of it. Additionally, if you place a wool block between a sensor and a vibration source, the wool acts as a dampener that prevents the sensor from detecting the vibration entirely. This also applies to the warden, which cannot detect any of these wool-related activities.

question: what do i get if i mine stone without a pickaxe?
answer: If you mine stone without using a pickaxe, it drops nothing.

question: how do i make stone if i want to build with it?
answer: You can obtain stone by mining it with a Silk Touch enchanted pickaxe. Alternatively, you can smelt cobblestone in a furnace using any fuel to turn it back into stone.

question: is there a way to get stone using water and lava?
answer: Yes, stone is a renewable resource. You can create it by placing lava so that it flows directly on top of water blocks, including both source blocks and flowing water. When the lava touches the water, the water is replaced by a stone block, which you can then mine with a pickaxe.

question: how do i make a cauldron
answer: You can craft a cauldron using 7 iron ingots in a crafting table.

question: can i use a cauldron to store lava and does it do anything special
answer: Yes, you can store lava in a cauldron by using a lava bucket on it. A lava-filled cauldron emits a light level of 15 and outputs a redstone signal strength of 3 when a comparator is placed behind it.

question: how do i get infinite lava using cauldrons
answer: You can make lava renewable by placing a pointed dripstone block underneath a lava source block, with a cauldron positioned directly below the dripstone. The dripstone will slowly drip lava into the cauldron, filling it over time. The cauldron can be placed up to 10 blocks below the dripstone, but it will not fill if there are any solid blocks or other materials blocking the space between them.

question: can i put silk touch on a sword
answer: No, you cannot apply Silk Touch to swords or fishing rods in normal gameplay.

question: what happens if i use silk touch and fortune on the same pickaxe
answer: Silk Touch and Fortune are incompatible enchantments. If you use commands to force both onto one item, Silk Touch will take precedence and prevent the Fortune effect from working on blocks affected by both.

question: why did my bee nest drop as an item but the bees disappeared
answer: If you are not using a tool with the Silk Touch enchantment, you will not get the bee nest as an item. When you successfully mine a bee nest with a Silk Touch tool, it will drop as an item and retain any bees that were inside, provided there were 1-3 bees present when you broke it.

question: how do i craft shears in minecraft
answer: You can craft shears by placing two iron ingots in a crafting grid.

question: do shears lose durability if i try to shear a sheep that has no wool
answer: No, shears do not lose durability when you attempt to shear a sheep that has no wool. Durability is only consumed if the shearing action is successful.

question: if i use a dispenser to harvest honeycombs from a beehive, will the bees get mad at me
answer: No, using a dispenser to harvest honeycombs does not anger the bees or cause them to leave the hive. This is different from shearing by hand, where you must place a campfire or soul campfire underneath the hive to prevent the bees from exiting and attacking you.

question: how do i make a block of diamond
answer: You can craft a block of diamond by placing 9 diamonds in a 3x3 grid in a crafting table.

question: what happens if i mine diamond ore with silk touch
answer: If you mine diamond ore with a pickaxe enchanted with Silk Touch, it will drop the ore block itself instead of a diamond. You will need to mine it with a regular iron pickaxe or better to get the diamond item.

question: is there a way to get more than one diamond from a single ore block
answer: Yes, you can get more diamonds by using a pickaxe enchanted with Fortune. Each level of Fortune increases the chance of dropping extra diamonds, allowing you to obtain up to 4 diamonds from a single ore block when using Fortune III. Keep in mind that you must use an iron pickaxe or better to mine the ore, otherwise it will drop nothing at all.

question: how do i make a new project in the bedrock editor?
answer: You can create a new project directly from the main menu of the Bedrock Editor. Once you are in the projects list, simply select the option to create a new project.

question: can i use the editor on a server and what happens to my world options?
answer: Yes, you can enable the Editor for clients on a Bedrock Dedicated Server by launching it with the 'Editor=true' command. Keep in mind that a project is not 'played' until exported, so world options like the bonus chest can still be toggled even after you have started editing.

question: why are my achievements disabled when i use the editor?
answer: Achievements are always disabled for any projects or worlds created with the Editor because the tool is designed for world creation and testing rather than standard survival gameplay. Since projects remain in an initial creation state until they are exported, they do not track progression in the same way a regular world does. You can still use the test world feature to play in survival mode, but those changes are not saved to the project file.

question: how do i get milk from a cow?
answer: You can milk an adult cow by using a bucket on it, which will give you a milk bucket.

question: why wont my baby cow grow up?
answer: If you feed a baby cow a golden dandelion, its growth will freeze and reset to the full 20-minute timer. You need to feed it another golden dandelion to resume its growth.

question: does the biome i breed cows in change what kind of baby they have?
answer: No, the variant of the baby cow is inherited directly from one of its parents. It does not matter what biome you are standing in when you breed them, as the offspring will simply take after the parent's variant.

question: how do i make a boat in minecraft
answer: to craft a boat, you need to combine five wooden planks of the same type in a crafting table. you can also add a chest to the recipe to create a boat with a chest.

question: what happens to my items if i drop them in water
answer: if you drop an item in water, it will float and ascend toward the surface. if the water is flowing, the item will be pushed along with the current.

question: how long do items stay on the ground before they disappear
answer: when an item is dropped as an entity, it will remain in a loaded chunk for 5 minutes before it despawns. however, this timer does not apply to the nether star, and items can be destroyed earlier if they touch fire, lava, cactus, explosions, or the void.

question: how do i get a grass block in survival
answer: You can obtain a grass block by mining it with a tool that has the Silk Touch enchantment. Alternatively, you can kill an enderman that is currently holding a grass block, and it will drop the block.

question: why does the grass in my base keep turning into dirt
answer: Grass blocks will die and turn into dirt if they are covered by opaque blocks. They can also die under transparent blocks if the light level directly above the grass block drops to four or lower.

question: what are the rules for grass spreading to dirt blocks
answer: For grass to spread, the dirt block must be within a 3x5x3 area around the source grass block. The source block needs a light level of 9 or higher directly above it, and the block immediately above the target dirt block must allow light to pass through. Grass can spread up to three levels downward, which is why it often moves down slopes faster than it climbs up them.

question: how do i craft a bow
answer: You can craft a bow by combining three sticks and three strings in a crafting table.

question: can i put mending and infinity on the same bow
answer: No, you cannot have both Mending and Infinity on the same bow as they are mutually exclusive enchantments.

question: why does my bow shake when i hold it drawn for a long time
answer: If you hold a bow at full charge for too long, it will begin to shake and lose its critical charge. This effect happens after holding the draw for 3 seconds, which resets your shot and forces you to re-draw to get the critical damage bonus again.

question: how do i tame a cat in minecraft
answer: You can tame a stray cat by feeding it raw cod or raw salmon. Each fish you feed the cat has a 1/3 chance of successfully taming it.

question: why do my cats keep sitting on my chests and beds
answer: Tamed cats that are not currently sitting have a behavior where they attempt to get on top of chests, active furnaces, or the foot of beds within a 4-block radius. Once they are on top, they often assume a sitting position on their own, which can make chests unusable until you command the cat to stand.

question: do cats teleport if they are sitting or in a boat
answer: Generally, cats do not teleport if they have been ordered to sit, though they might if they are injured while sitting. They also will not teleport if they are currently inside a boat or minecart, if they are in an unloaded chunk, or if they are attached to a fence post with a lead. Additionally, they won't teleport if the area around the player lacks a valid space, such as a 5x5x1 region with a transparent block for the cat to stand on.

question: how do i make a map in minecraft
answer: To craft a map, you need to place paper in the crafting grid. If you are playing in Bedrock Edition, you can craft an empty map with just one paper in a cartography table, or use paper and a compass to make an empty locator map.

question: can i zoom out a map that is already locked
answer: No, you cannot zoom out a map once it has been locked. Locking a map using a glass pane in a cartography table makes it permanent, meaning it will never change even if you alter the terrain in the world.

question: why does my map look weird and show a red-and-gray pattern in the nether
answer: Maps in the Nether are not designed to show terrain like they do in the Overworld, so they display a red-and-gray pattern instead. The player pointer will also spin rapidly, making it unreliable for navigation. However, it can still be useful for tracking your location relative to framed copies of the same map, which appear as green pointers, or for marking trails while riding a strider over lava.

question: what animals only spawn in the jungle biome?
answer: Parrots, ocelots, and pandas are the animals that exclusively spawn in jungle biomes.

question: can i find jungle pyramids in every jungle biome?
answer: Jungle pyramids generate in regular jungles, but in Java Edition, they can also generate in bamboo jungles. They are made of mossy cobblestone and vines, which can make them difficult to spot due to the dense tree cover.

question: why does my game lag so much when i explore the jungle?
answer: Jungle biomes are significantly more resource-demanding to load and render than other biomes because of their dense vegetation and complex features. If you are experiencing low frame rates or lag while exploring, you may want to turn down your graphics settings to help the game run more smoothly.

question: do husks burn in the sun like normal zombies?
answer: No, husks are immune to burning in sunlight, which allows them to remain active during the day.

question: why do i get hungry when fighting husks?
answer: Husks apply the Hunger effect whenever they land a melee attack on you. However, you can prevent this effect by blocking their attacks with a shield or by ensuring the husk is holding an item in its main hand.

question: what happens if a husk stays in water for a long time?
answer: If a husk is fully submerged in water for 30 seconds, it begins a 15-second conversion process into a normal zombie. Once this process finishes, the resulting zombie will have full health regardless of how much damage the husk had taken previously, and it cannot be stopped even if the mob leaves the water.

question: how do i make redstone dust from a block of redstone?
answer: You can craft redstone dust by placing a block of redstone into a crafting grid, which will yield 9 pieces of redstone dust.

question: whats the difference between mining redstone ore with silk touch vs fortune?
answer: If you mine redstone ore with a Silk Touch pickaxe, the block drops itself. If you use a Fortune pickaxe, it drops 4 or 5 redstone dust, with Fortune III averaging about 6 dust per block.

question: why does my redstone wire sometimes stay powered even when i turn the lever off?
answer: Redstone wire does not always update surrounding components when it reconfigures after you place or change it. If the wire configuration changes without a power level update, a mechanism might stay stuck in its previous state until it receives a block update from another source, which is a quirk often used to build block update detectors.

question: how do i find a stronghold in my world?
answer: You can locate a stronghold by using an eye of ender. These items will guide you to the structure's location.

question: do strongholds generate differently in bedrock vs java?
answer: Yes, they differ significantly. In Java Edition, there are 128 strongholds arranged in specific rings around the world origin, while in Bedrock Edition, they generate randomly throughout the world with no set limit, though they are always at least 160 blocks from the origin.

question: why can't i find the portal room in my stronghold?
answer: The portal room is guaranteed to generate, but it never appears within five rooms of the starting staircase. If the generation process fails to create a portal room, the entire structure is discarded and the game restarts the generation process, so it is possible you are exploring a stronghold that simply hasn't finished its layout or you missed a path branching from the five-way crossings.

question: how do i make a crafting table
answer: You can craft a crafting table by arranging four wooden planks in a 2x2 square within your inventory crafting grid.

question: can i move items around in a shaped recipe
answer: Yes, you can move ingredients in a shaped recipe up, down, left, or right, and you can even flip them horizontally. However, you cannot flip them vertically, and some specific recipes are fixed and cannot be moved or mirrored at all.

question: how do hoppers interact with the crafter
answer: Hoppers can automatically insert items into a crafter to fill its slots. Items are placed into the crafter's enabled slots in order from left to right and top to bottom. You can disable specific slots to control where items go, but if all slots are disabled, hoppers will not be able to put any items into the crafter.

question: how do i make tnt in minecraft
answer: You can craft TNT by combining 5 gunpowder with 4 sand or red sand in a crafting table. You can use any combination of sand and red sand to make the recipe work.

question: does tnt explode if i put it in water
answer: Generally, TNT cannot destroy blocks while underwater. However, in Java Edition, you can make it work if you place the TNT on top of a block that is less than a full block high, like a soul sand or honey block, before activating it.

question: why does my tnt sometimes blow up way faster than usual
answer: The time it takes for TNT to go off depends on how it was triggered. If you light it with redstone or flint and steel, it takes 80 game ticks, or 4 seconds, to explode. But if the TNT is set off by another explosion, the fuse is randomized to between 10 and 30 ticks, which is only 0.5 to 1.5 seconds.

question: how do i make coarse dirt
answer: You can craft coarse dirt by combining two blocks of dirt and two blocks of gravel in a crafting grid.

question: why does my farmland keep turning back into dirt
answer: Farmland will revert to dirt if a mob jumps on it, if you place a solid block on top of it, or if nothing is planted on it and it is not within four blocks of water.

question: is there a way to get infinite dirt without just digging up the whole world
answer: Yes, you can make dirt renewable by using moss blocks. By tilling coarse dirt, you can turn gravel into dirt, and since gravel is renewable through piglin bartering, this provides a steady supply. Alternatively, you can grow azalea trees to convert moss into rooted dirt, or use bone meal to turn stone into moss, which can then be processed into dirt-related blocks.

question: how do i craft a sword in minecraft?
answer: To craft a sword, you need one stick and two of the material corresponding to the tier you want, such as wooden planks, stone-tier blocks, iron ingots, or diamonds. Place the stick in the bottom-center slot of the crafting grid and the two materials in the center and top-center slots.

question: is it better to use a sword or my hand to break bamboo?
answer: You should use a sword because it breaks bamboo instantly, which is much faster than using your bare hands. However, keep in mind that using a sword to break bamboo will decrease the sword's durability by 2 points for each block broken.

question: why did my sweep attack not hit the mobs standing right next to the one i was fighting?
answer: In Java Edition, a sweep attack only triggers if you are standing on the ground, not sprinting, and your attack recharge meter is at least 84.8% full. Additionally, the surrounding mobs must have their feet within 3 blocks of your feet and be located within a specific 1 by 0.25 by 1 block area relative to the mob you hit. If you were moving, jumping, or attacking too quickly, the sweep effect would not activate.

question: how do i get a spawn egg in survival mode?
answer: you actually cannot obtain spawn eggs in survival mode. they are only available through the creative menu or by using commands.

question: if i use a spawn egg on a mob, does it do anything special?
answer: if you use a spawn egg on the same type of mob it spawns, it will create a baby version of that mob. this works for most mobs, but it does not work for camel husks.

question: do renamed spawn eggs keep mobs from despawning?
answer: no, renaming a spawn egg on an anvil will make the mob spawn with that name over its head, but it does not prevent hostile mobs from despawning like a name tag would. you can only see the name if you are within four blocks of the mob, and the name will show up in death messages if the mob kills you.

question: what does the luck attribute actually do in minecraft?
answer: The luck attribute affects the results of loot tables, such as when you are fishing, opening chests, or killing mobs that use the quality or bonus_rolls tags.

question: how do i make a zombie that follows me from further away?
answer: You can change how far a mob tracks you by using the follow_range attribute. When summoning a zombie, you can use the command /summon zombie ~ ~ ~ {attributes:[{id:"follow_range", base:100.0d}]} to make it follow you from up to 100 blocks away instead of the default 35.

question: if i have multiple modifiers on an item, how does the game calculate the final value?
answer: The game applies modifiers in a specific order: first it processes all add_value modifiers, then all add_multiplied_base modifiers, and finally all add_multiplied_total modifiers. The total value calculated after each step is carried over as the base for the next operation. Note that if you have two modifiers with the exact same ID affecting the same attribute, they will not stack; the most recently added one will simply override the previous one.

question: do ghasts drop anything when they die?
answer: Yes, when killed, ghasts drop ghast tears and gunpowder. They also drop a music disc called 'Music Disc Tears' if you kill them by hitting their own fireball back at them.

question: can i use smite on a ghast to kill it faster?
answer: No, smite does not work on ghasts because they are not considered undead mobs. Even though they look like ghosts, they can drown, be poisoned, and are healed by instant health potions.

question: why do ghasts sometimes ignore me even when i am right in front of them?
answer: Ghasts require a clear line of sight to target you, which can be blocked by any solid or transparent block. If you are in Java Edition, they only target players within 64 blocks horizontally and 4 blocks vertically. Additionally, they are completely ignored by several other mobs like wolves, snow golems, and zoglins, so they might be focused on something else or simply unable to see you through the environment.

question: how do i make glass in minecraft
answer: You can make glass by smelting sand or red sand in a furnace using any fuel.

question: can i pick up glass after i place it down
answer: No, if you break glass with a normal tool or your hand, it will just break and drop nothing. You must use a tool enchanted with Silk Touch to get the glass block back as an item.

question: why do my redstone signals act weird when i put them on glass
answer: Glass behaves like a top slab in redstone circuits. While you can place redstone dust and components on top of it, the glass cannot be powered by them. Additionally, in Java Edition, glass will transmit redstone signals upward but not downward, which is why your circuits might not be working as expected.

question: what do i need to feed rabbits to breed them?
answer: You can breed rabbits by using carrots, golden carrots, or dandelions.

question: why do my rabbits keep running away from me?
answer: Rabbits are naturally skittish and will avoid players within an 8-block radius unless you are holding one of their favorite foods, which are carrots, golden carrots, or dandelions. If you aren't holding these items, they will flee when you get too close.

question: how do i get a rabbit to have the toast skin?
answer: To get a Toast rabbit, you must rename a rabbit to 'Toast' using a name tag or a renamed spawn egg. This changes its appearance to a black dutch pattern with specific markings around the face. Note that this is purely cosmetic, and if you breed a Toast rabbit, the offspring will not inherit the Toast pattern but will instead have a color based on the parents' original fur types.

question: how do i put a chest on a donkey?
answer: You can equip a donkey with a chest by simply holding a chest and using it on the donkey. Once equipped, it provides 15 inventory slots for storing items.

question: can i breed a donkey with a horse and what happens?
answer: Yes, you can breed a tamed donkey with a horse by feeding them golden apples or golden carrots to enter love mode. This will produce a baby mule, which is a unique mob that cannot breed.

question: why does my donkey take less fall damage than my horse?
answer: Donkeys have a unique trait where they can safely fall up to 6 blocks before they start taking damage, whereas most other mobs can only fall 3 blocks safely. Additionally, when they do take fall damage, they only take half the normal amount, calculated as 0.5 HP per block over the safe distance. If you are riding the donkey, you will receive the same reduced fall damage as the animal.

question: are there any trees in the desert biome?
answer: No, deserts are barren and do not have trees. You can only get wood logs by finding pillager outposts, mineshafts, or by finding an azalea tree if there is a lush cave underground.

question: why do zombies and skeletons act weird in my desert base at night?
answer: In deserts, most zombies are replaced by husks, which inflict the Hunger effect, and most skeletons are replaced by parched, which shoot arrows of weakness. Unlike regular mobs, these variants do not burn in the sun, so they can stay active during the day.

question: can i find lush caves under a desert?
answer: Yes, but only under specific conditions. Lush caves can generate underneath deserts if the humidity value is high enough. However, because deserts have a high erosion value, the deep dark biome is prevented from generating underneath them.

question: how do i get shulker shells to make shulker boxes
answer: You can obtain shulker shells by killing shulkers, which are found in End cities. They have a 50% base chance to drop a shell when killed.

question: do shulkers respawn if i kill them all in an end city
answer: No, shulkers do not naturally respawn once they are killed. However, they can duplicate if a shulker is hit by another shulker's bullet while its lid is open, provided certain conditions like health and proximity to other shulkers are met.

question: why did my shulker stop duplicating when i was trying to farm them
answer: A shulker will stop attempting to duplicate if it has less than 50% health, as it will prioritize teleporting away instead. Additionally, the duplication chance is reduced by 20% for every other shulker within an 8-block radius, meaning if there are 5 or more shulkers nearby, the duplication check will always fail. Finally, the shulker must be able to successfully teleport to a valid adjacent block for the process to complete.

question: how do i make a block of coal
answer: You can craft a block of coal by placing 9 pieces of coal into a crafting grid.

question: is it better to burn coal or a block of coal in my furnace
answer: Using a block of coal is more efficient for smelting large amounts of items. While one block is made from 9 coal, it lasts 10 times as long as a single piece of coal would.

question: does fortune actually help when mining coal ore
answer: Yes, using a pickaxe enchanted with Fortune increases the amount of coal you get from mining coal ore. You can receive one extra piece of coal per level of the enchantment, which means you can get a maximum of 4 pieces of coal from a single ore block if you are using Fortune III.

question: how do i open the advancements menu in java edition?
answer: You can open the advancements screen by pressing the L key on your keyboard, or by selecting the button for it in the pause menu.

question: do i get anything special for finishing a challenge advancement?
answer: Yes, completing a challenge advancement rewards you with experience points. You can identify these advancements in the interface because they have pink header text and purple descriptions.

question: why cant i see the how did we get here advancement in my menu?
answer: This is a hidden advancement, which means it remains invisible in your menu until you have actually completed it. It does not matter if you have finished any of the child advancements or other branches; the game keeps it secret until you successfully apply all 34 required status effects at the same time.

question: how do i make leather from rabbit hide
answer: You can craft leather by placing 4 rabbit hides into a crafting grid.

question: does looting help get more leather from cows
answer: Yes, using a sword with Looting increases the amount of leather dropped. While a cow normally drops 0–2 leather, a Looting III sword increases the drop range to 0–5.

question: is it better to kill hoglins or cows for leather
answer: Cows are generally more reliable for leather because they have a 66.67% drop chance, whereas hoglins only have a 50% chance. Additionally, the expected number of kills to get a specific amount of leather is lower for cows compared to hoglins.
